title: Ring
statement:
A \emph{ring} is a triple $(R, +, \cdot)$ where $R$ is a set and
$+$ and $\cdot$ are binary operations on $R$ satisfying:

- [label=\textbf{R\arabic*.}]
- \textbf{Additive abelian group.}
 $(R, +)$ is an abelian group:
 - - $(a + b) + c = a + (b + c)$ for all $a,b,c \in R$.
- $a + b = b + a$ for all $a, b \in R$.
- There exists $0 \in R$ such that $a + 0 = a$ for all $a \in R$.
- For each $a \in R$, there exists $-a \in R$ such that
 $a + (-a) = 0$.
- \textbf{Multiplicative associativity.}
 $(a \cdot b) \cdot c = a \cdot (b \cdot c)$
 for all $a, b, c \in R$.
- \textbf{Distributivity.}
 - - $a \cdot (b + c) = a \cdot b + a \cdot c$
 for all $a,b,c \in R$.
- $(a + b) \cdot c = a \cdot c + b \cdot c$
 for all $a,b,c \in R$.
